text: When editing aims, enter on most input fields (e.g. title, status, but not textareas) should save changes and close modal
description: And esc on most fields except e.g. textarea (because enter is used there for newline) should close the modal. I think this works in new aim but not edit aim modal.

reflections.context: Confirm and lock in edit-aim Enter/Escape behavior, especially Enter-to-save on non-textarea fields.
reflections.outcome: Added focused tests in AimCreationModal.spec.ts and verified behavior via unit tests; no modal logic patch was required because implementation already matched intended behavior.
reflections.effectiveness: High: regression coverage now protects title/status select and textarea semantics.
reflections.lesson: When behavior is disputed, start by codifying expectations in UI tests before changing code.
reflections.pattern: Recurring pattern: interaction regressions are best stabilized with direct component-level key event tests.

reflections.context: Enter-to-save still failed during real editing despite prior tests.
reflections.outcome: Identified mismatch: issue was in AimEditModal (used in real edit flow), where a window capture keydown listener blocked modal input key handlers. Removed interception and added dedicated AimEditModal keyboard tests.
reflections.effectiveness: High: fix is minimal, targeted, and backed by regression tests for both single-line and textarea behavior.
reflections.lesson: Validate the actually wired component path in App before trusting coverage from a similarly named modal.
reflections.pattern: Recurring pattern in UI regressions: global keyboard interception in capture phase can silently break local field handlers.

status.comment: Fixed true root cause in AimEditModal: removed global window keydown capture stopPropagation that blocked field-level Enter handlers. Added regression tests for Enter save on title and Ctrl+Enter behavior for textarea. Verified unit tests and typecheck.
